articles.title: Chaos returns to Middle East: Oil prices jump 4% as US-Iran exchange strikes
articles.description: International Business News: Oil prices soared sharply on Monday as the Middle East was once again embroiled in chaos. Crude prices jumped 4% as fresh military action between Iran.

articles.title: Gold slides over 1% as oil surges on Strait of Hormuz closure fears
articles.description: Gold prices declined over one percent in early Asian trade Monday. Oil prices jumped sharply, increasing inflation concerns and interest rate expectations. U.S. and Iranian forces exchanged missile and drone assaults in the Gulf region. Federal Reserve chair testimony and economic data will offer policy clues. Gold speculators reduced net long positions while China increased its reserves.

articles.title: Fresh US strikes hit Iran amid renewed fighting over Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: US forces retaliated with strikes after Iran attacked shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, escalating conflict that threatens peace talks and impacts.Strait of Hormuz, US military strikes Iran, Iran conflict, Middle East tensions, oil prices, global economy, Kuwait attack, Iranian Revolutionary Guards, maritime security, diplomatic solution, international relations, Middle East politics, conflict escalation, navigation freedom, conflict aftermath

articles.title: Dollar jumps on renewed Middle East attacks, Hormuz closure
articles.description: The dollar strengthened against most currencies due to renewed Middle East conflict. This conflict fueled inflation fears and increased rate hike prospects from central banks. Oil prices rose significantly, driving the dollar's upward movement and inflation concerns. Traders now anticipate two Federal Reserve rate hikes by year-end. Upcoming U.S. inflation data and Fed testimony will remain key focus areas.

articles.title: Crude oil prices jump 3% amid renewed US-Iran war tensions; Brent crude above $78 per barrel
articles.description: US-Iran war: Brent crude climbed 3% above $78 a barrel after gaining 5.4% last week, while US benchmark West Texas Intermediate hovered near $74.

articles.title: Iran fires ballistic missiles towards Jordan as US strike kills one; oil prices surge
articles.description: Iran fired at least 12 ballistic missiles towards Jordan in two waves as the latest exchange of attacks with the US sent global oil prices sharply higher.

articles.title: Trump Directs Fresh Strikes to 'Hold Iranian Forces Accountable,' Says CENTCOM; Oil Tops $74 as Dow Futur
articles.description: Trump ordered fresh strikes on Iran, sending oil prices higher and U.S. stock futures lower as Strait of Hormuz tensions escalated.

articles.title: Gift Nifty, US-Iran war, bond yields to crude oil prices: 10 things that changed for Indian stock market over weekend
articles.description: Gift Nifty was trading around 24,056 level, a discount of nearly 186 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close, indicating a gap-down start for the Indian stock market indices.

articles.title: Indian rupee and bonds likely to track Mideast developments, inflation data
articles.description: The Indian rupee and government bonds will likely track developments in the Middle East conflict alongside inflation data from the U.S. and India to gauge the impact of volatile oil prices on broader costs and central bank policy decisions.

articles.title: Asian stocks today: Kospi, Nikkei decline following renewed US-Iran war tensions, rising crude oil prices
articles.description: Asian stocks today: Japan's Nikkei 225 fell 0.2%, while the broader Topix index declined 0.094%. South Korea's Kospi was down 2.47%, with the small-cap Kosdaq climbing 0.98%
